name: Claus Ogerman
abstract: Claus Ogerman (born Klaus Ogermann; 29 April 1930 – 8 March 2016) was a German arranger, conductor, and composer best known for his work with Billie Holiday, Antonio Carlos Jobim, Frank Sinatra, Bill Evans, Michael Brecker, Michael Franks, Barbra Streisand, and Diana Krall.

about_quotes.speaker: Anne Phillips
about_quotes.evidence: And this was Claus Ogerman, there we are. And Claus had his hands up in the air and I’m in the booth with my hands up ready to cue the singers
about_quotes.doc_title: Anne Phillips interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 24, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Warren Bernhardt
about_quotes.evidence: if it was Claus Ogerman or something you’d know a month in advance... to look over the strange chords and the harmonies
about_quotes.doc_title: Warren Bernhardt interviewed by Monk Rowe, Bearsville, New York, January 3, 2012

about_quotes.speaker: Bruce Johnstone
about_quotes.evidence: And when I first started writing, that was the model that was taken. Him, Alan Broadbent, Claus Ogerman.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bruce Johnstone interviewed by Monk Rowe, Dunkirk, New York, October 11, 2013

about_quotes.speaker: George Benson
about_quotes.evidence: Munich Symphony Orchestra was where Claus Ogerman, who wrote the arrangements, was.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Benson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Roger Kellaway
about_quotes.evidence: He said, 'Oh I don't know, somewhere between Gil Evans and Claus Ogerman.'
about_quotes.doc_title: Roger Kellaway interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Marty Morell
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...you played with him and you played with Don Sebesky...
about_quotes.doc_title: Marty Morell interviewed by Monk Rowe, Orlando, Florida, February 24, 2017
